title: VPN on the Asus RT-AX88U: Setup, Which VPNs Work, and Real Limitations
description: A practical guide to running a VPN on the Asus RT-AX88U. Covers WireGuard vs OpenVPN on stock firmware, Merlin firmware advantages, actual throughput numbers, and which commercial VPNs work reliably.
content:
## What the RT-AX88U Can Actually Do

The Asus RT-AX88U is a dual-band Wi-Fi 6 router with a 1.8 GHz quad-core ARM Cortex-A53 processor and 1 GB of RAM. That hardware is capable enough to run a VPN at useful speeds, but there are real ceilings, and knowing them before you set anything up saves a lot of frustration.

Stock ASUSWRT and the third-party Merlin firmware both support VPN, but they are not equivalent. Stock firmware gives you OpenVPN client and server, IPsec, and, on firmware 3.0.0.4.388.23000 or later, WireGuard via VPN Fusion. Merlin adds finer-grained policy routing, more OpenVPN options, and a cleaner WireGuard client experience.

## Firmware Version Matters

Before touching VPN settings, check your firmware version. Go to **Administration > Firmware Upgrade** and confirm you are on at least `3.0.0.4.388.23000`. WireGuard is not available on anything older. If you are running Merlin, the equivalent milestone is Merlin 388.1, released in late 2022, which added native WireGuard support.

To check on Merlin: SSH into the router and run `nvram get buildno`. The output should read 388 or higher.

## WireGuard Setup on Stock Firmware

1. Log into the router at `192.168.1.1`.
2. Go to **VPN > VPN Client**.
3. Click **Add profile**, select **WireGuard**.
4. Paste in your VPN provider's WireGuard config file. NordVPN, Mullvad, ProtonVPN, and IVPN all export these from their account dashboards.
5. Set **Auto-start at boot** to ON.
6. Under **VPN Fusion**, you can assign specific devices to tunnel through the VPN while others use the regular connection. This is the RT-AX88U's version of split tunneling.

The WireGuard interface on the router will appear as `wg0` internally. You can confirm it is running via SSH: `wg show`.

## OpenVPN Setup

1. Go to **VPN > VPN Client > Add profile > OpenVPN**.
2. Import the `.ovpn` file from your VPN provider.
3. Enter your credentials if required (NordVPN uses a service credential separate from your login password).
4. Enable the connection.

OpenVPN on the RT-AX88U works with most commercial providers. The main downside is speed.

## Actual Throughput Numbers

This is where things get honest. The RT-AX88U can push around 950 Mbps WAN-to-LAN without any VPN. Add a VPN and the picture changes significantly.

**WireGuard throughput:** 250-300 Mbps through the tunnel. Non-VPN traffic drops to around 400 Mbps because WireGuard disables hardware NAT acceleration on this router. This is a known limitation of the ASUSWRT WireGuard implementation, not a WireGuard problem in general.

**OpenVPN throughput:** 80-120 Mbps, depending on cipher. AES-128-GCM is faster than AES-256-CBC. If your internet connection is under 100 Mbps, OpenVPN is fine. Above that, you will hit the ceiling.

If you have a gigabit fiber connection and want to route all traffic through a VPN, the RT-AX88U is not the right tool. For 300 Mbps or less, WireGuard on this router is perfectly capable.

## Which VPNs Work Best on the RT-AX88U

**NordVPN** works well via both WireGuard (NordLynx config files) and OpenVPN. NordVPN exports WireGuard configs per server from the manual configuration section of the account dashboard. One limitation: NordVPN rotates WireGuard keys periodically, so your config file can stop working after a few weeks. You need to generate a new one from the dashboard.

**Mullvad** exports clean WireGuard configs with no account login required on the router itself. Mullvad also supports port forwarding, which matters if you run a server behind the router.

**ProtonVPN** exports WireGuard configs but requires a paid plan. Free ProtonVPN accounts do not support router-level WireGuard.

**ExpressVPN** uses its own Lightway protocol, which is not natively supported on ASUSWRT. They offer a dedicated router app for some ASUS models, but the RT-AX88U is not on their officially supported list. OpenVPN works as a fallback.

**Surfshark** and **IPVanish** both work via OpenVPN on stock firmware.

## VPN Fusion: Per-Device Routing

One of the more useful RT-AX88U features is VPN Fusion, which lets you run multiple VPN connections simultaneously and assign specific devices (by MAC address) to each one. A laptop can tunnel through NordVPN while a smart TV goes through a different connection or the regular WAN.

To configure this: **VPN > VPN Fusion**, add your connections, then use the device routing table to assign clients.

## Known Limitations

- WireGuard on this router breaks hardware NAT acceleration for all traffic, not just the VPN tunnel. You lose roughly 50% of your non-VPN throughput.
- The OpenVPN implementation does not support multi-hop or custom routing tables without Merlin.
- IPv6 leak protection is not automatic. If your VPN does not handle IPv6, disable IPv6 on the router entirely under **IPv6 > Connection Type: Disabled**.
- VPN Fusion does not support WireGuard kill switches per device. If the tunnel drops, affected devices fall back to the plain WAN.

## Recommendation

For most home setups on the RT-AX88U, WireGuard via NordVPN's manual config files gives you the best balance of speed and ease of use. NordVPN's Linux and router WireGuard configs are well-documented, key rotation is manageable, and their server network is large enough to find low-latency servers in most regions. If you want a VPN that works reliably on routers without constant maintenance, [NordVPN](https://nordvpn.com) is the strongest choice for this hardware.
